key_insight: ZO-Act improves zeroth-order fine-tuning by searching in a fixed activation-derived low-rank subspace instead of perturbing full weights or random subspaces, reducing estimator noise and enabling efficient forward-only training.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to fine-tune large and quantized LLMs when backpropagation is unavailable or too memory-intensive, while remaining compatible with standard optimizers like Adam.
method: Compute a small activation basis once at initialization for each linear layer.
Optimize only lightweight coefficient matrices in the low-rank subspace using forward-only loss evaluations.
Keep low-bit weights frozen to support quantized LLM fine-tuning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B across language understanding, question answering, and commonsense reasoning benchmarks.
watch_item: The approach introduces a subspace approximation bias, so performance may depend on how well the chosen activation basis captures the relevant gradient directions.
paper_title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
